key_themes: Record revenue growth (120% YoY to $16.4M in Q2 FY26) with expanding gross margins to 37%
Strategic role as US supplier of Black Diamond IR optics replacing China-dependent Germanium amid NDAA and defense demand
Robust ~$100M backlog, acquisitions (Amorphous Materials, G5), capital raise, and accelerated profitability targets
Consistent analyst Buy ratings with price targets raised to $14–$15.50
Strong technical momentum, new 52-week highs near $15.24, and active trader interest in photonics/defense small-caps
sources: 25+ news articles, earnings reports, transcripts, and analyst notes (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Seeking Alpha, PR Newswire, company IR)
20+ recent X posts (momentum traders, defense-tech analysts, April 2026 discussions)
summary: LPTH sentiment is strongly bullish following record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $16.4M (up 120% YoY), gross profit surging 212%, improved 37% margins, and a near-$100M backlog fueled by defense, drone, and infrared imaging demand.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/LPTH/)[[2]](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lightpath-technologies-reports-fiscal-2026-second-quarter-financial-results-302685505.html) Multiple analysts maintain Buy ratings with upward revisions to $14–$15.50 targets, citing its proprietary Black Diamond glass as a critical, ITAR-compliant alternative to Chinese Germanium amid supply chain mandates.[[3]](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/LPTH) The stock has risen over 600% in the past year, recently hitting new highs around $15.24 with sustained positive X chatter on its photonics positioning and growth runway, though profitability remains a work in progress amid acquisition costs.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/LPTH/history/)
